current_field_values.paragraph_anchor: L3a (PRED-095, magnetic) — sub-tests, goalpost scope, Fail-clause double-bind

replacement_field_values.old_sentence: The dome also publishes four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, E-PRED-D non-path stations below noise floor) for the August 2026 eclipse; these are evaluated in Section 4.2 Eclipse and in Section 9 and do not change the non-discrimination assessment of the magnitude-only W004 cluster.
replacement_field_values.new_sentence: The dome’s 2026-03-12 predictions snapshot (preserved in raw-text/03-predictions.txt) registered four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests for the August 2026 eclipse: E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, E-PRED-D non-path stations below noise floor. As of our 2026-05-07 fetch, only E-PRED-B remains explicitly labeled on the live page (“E-PRED-B LOCKED”); A, C, and D have been folded without WITHDRAWN notation into the dome’s restructured Tier 1/2/3 κ-coupling protocol. Most consequentially, E-PRED-C inverted between snapshots — the 2026-03-12 page predicted SG gravimeters would read 0.0 µGal during eclipse contact phase; the 2026-05-07 live page replaces it with PRED-TIER-3’s positive 10–13 µGal pulse for the same instrument class at the same August 2026 eclipse (see WIN-014 for the resulting self-contradiction analysis). We continue to evaluate all four registered sub-tests because the 2026-03-12 enumeration is preserved in our raw-text archive and the post-snapshot restructuring does not retire the prediction commitments; they are evaluated in Section 4.2 Eclipse and in Section 9 and do not change the non-discrimination assessment of the magnitude-only W004 cluster.

verification_performed.live_page_eclipse_protocol_structure: Tier 1 (coherent magnetic signal at >=3 stations, r > 0.5) / Tier 2 (co-located gravimeter records) / Tier 3 (SG contact-phase 10-13 µGal pulse), with E-PRED-B "LOCKED" appearing twice (lines ~98 and ~124 of the live HTML) inside the Tier 1 magnetic protocol; PRED-TIER-3 explicitly states 10-13 µGal positive pulse, the inverse of the 2026-03-12 E-PRED-C 0.0 µGal prediction.

holes_addressed: WIN-014.c4 / H1-PERSISTS (carried over from c3 H1, severity major): SEC-8b.3 enumerates E-PRED-A/B/C/D as live dome commitments while WIN-014 detail_evidence (now verdict Self-Contradicted) asserts E-PRED-C is absent from the current dome predictions page. The two surfaces were inconsistent. EXP-292 closes the cross-page inconsistency by anchoring the SEC-8b.3 enumeration to the 2026-03-12 raw-text snapshot date and recording the 2026-05-07 live-page restructuring (only E-PRED-B labelled; A/C/D folded into Tier 1/2/3 protocol).

ISS-1825 (decider-assigned): "SEC-8b.3 lists four E-PRED sub-tests including E-PRED-C, but WIN-014 detail_evidence notes the current predictions.html has no prediction labeled E-PRED-C. Reconcile by adding a parenthetical noting the raw-text snapshot date and re-verifying, OR stripping E-PRED-C if verification confirms removal." Resolved by chosen path: keep the enumeration anchored to the 2026-03-12 snapshot (which is preserved in raw-text/), and add the 2026-05-07 verification result as inline prose. Stripping was rejected — the four sub-tests were registered and we evaluate them; pruning our analysis would be a goalpost concession to the dome’s post-snapshot restructuring.
new_evidence_added: 2026-05-07 fresh fetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html (live dome page): only "E-PRED-B LOCKED" appears as an explicit E-PRED label; the page is now structured around a Tier 1/2/3 κ-coupling protocol with TIER 3 (SG Contact-Phase κ Test) explicitly predicting "10–13 µGal downward pulse in Level-3 public residuals at C1/C4" (line 138 of the fetched HTML). This is the inverse of the 2026-03-12 E-PRED-C "SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal" prediction for the same instrument class at the same eclipse.

raw-text/03-predictions.txt extracted 2026-03-12 (SOURCE: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html, lines 37–40): the four E-PREDs were enumerated on the live page on that date. This snapshot is preserved in our repository and is now the authoritative citation for the four-sub-test enumeration.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 2026-03-12 enumeration was a draft; we restructured the protocol to be cleaner. The Tier 1/2/3 protocol is the locked version. Pinning us to the 2026-03-12 draft is unfair.
anticipated_objections.response: The 2026-03-12 page was published — not internal — and was registered with the dome’s own "Cryptographically timestamped — falsifiable — pre-registered" framing at the top of predictions.html. Pruning sub-tests between snapshots without an explicit WITHDRAWN notation is precisely the goalpost-shift pattern we document elsewhere on the review (Timestamp Error tab). Moreover, the substance is preserved: the new SEC-8b.3 sentence does not pin the dome to a withdrawn label; it simply records both snapshots accurately and notes that PRED-TIER-3 carries forward (and inverts) the SG contact-phase commitment that E-PRED-C registered.

anticipated_objections.objection: E-PRED-C wasn’t inverted; it was replaced by a more discriminating test as the model was refined. Calling this an "inversion" misrepresents normal scientific iteration.
anticipated_objections.response: Refinement that flips a 0.0 µGal prediction to a 10–13 µGal prediction in the same instrument class for the same August 2026 eclipse is by definition a sign-flip on the predicted observable. Both predictions cannot be vindicated by the same eclipse data; whichever PRED is observed, the other is refuted. Our SEC-8b.3 sentence records the bare factual change (0.0 µGal in the 2026-03-12 snapshot vs 10–13 µGal in the 2026-05-07 page), then defers the deeper self-contradiction analysis to WIN-014, which is verdicted Self-Contradicted on exactly this ground.

anticipated_objections.objection: Maybe E-PRED-C content survives on the live page in some other form, just not labelled.
anticipated_objections.response: We searched the live page (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html, fetched 2026-05-07) for the substantive content of each E-PRED label: "0.0 µGal" appears only inside the Tier 3 protocol context (which predicts 10–13 µGal, not 0.0); "peak tracks geometry" / "non-path stations" / "below noise floor" appear nowhere on the live page. Only "E-PRED-B LOCKED" is preserved. The retired labels have not been silently re-encoded; they are absent.

anticipated_objections.objection: You are weaponising a benign restructuring as a cross-page inconsistency. The decider and curmudgeon should focus on substantive errors, not enumeration drift.
anticipated_objections.response: The fix here is minimal — three sentences replacing one — and the substantive content of SEC-8b.3 is preserved. What the patch buys us: (a) cross-page consistency with WIN-014’s detail_evidence, eliminating an exploitable inconsistency a dome defender could weaponise; (b) accurate dating of dome commitments, which is the entire point of our Timestamp Error tab thesis; (c) a documented goalpost shift (4 → 1 explicit E-PREDs over ~8 weeks) that a careful reader will notice anyway.

kernel_of_truth: The dome’s 2026-03-12 eclipse battery WAS a multi-test discrimination attempt — four named sub-tests targeting different observables (geometry tracking, station ordering, gravity, off-path noise) is a structurally serious approach. A model that registers four falsifiers, three of which are non-discriminating and one of which (E-PRED-C) self-contradicts a sister WIN, is not the same as a model that registers no falsifiers. We should give credit for the registration as a structural matter while documenting accurately what each sub-test actually tests. Why this still does not save the claim: per the part4 §4.2.4 analysis already in the review, three of the four (A, C, D) are non-discriminating or self-contradicting; only E-PRED-B (Hartland > Ebro) makes a prediction not trivially produced by standard physics — and even E-PRED-B reduces to a trigonometric tautology under our analysis. The post-snapshot restructuring has confined the dome’s public commitment to only the strongest (and weakest-discriminating-when-analysed) of the original four. Documenting this faithfully is more powerful than either pretending the four were never there or pretending they are still all live.

parallel_issue_flag.description: A parallel — but separate — instance of the same problem exists in data/sections.json:part4.html under the section <details id="p4-424"> ("4.2.4 The four E-PRED tests"). That section presents per-sub-test analysis for E-PRED-A, B, C, and D as if all four are currently published as labels. The same 2026-03-12 snapshot vs 2026-05-07 live-page divergence applies. Recommend the decider open a separate issue (cross-section consistency: SEC-4.2.4 + SEC-8b.3 + WIN-014) for the next analyst cycle. Do NOT bundle into EXP-292.
parallel_issue_flag.suggested_issue_text: SEC-4.2.4 (data/sections.json:part4.html, p4-424) and SEC-8b.3 (data/sections.json:part8b.html) both treat E-PRED-A/B/C/D as currently labelled commitments. As of 2026-05-07 the live dome page only labels E-PRED-B; A, C, D are folded into the Tier 1/2/3 protocol. EXP-292 patched SEC-8b.3. SEC-4.2.4 still needs an analogous patch (preserve the per-sub-test analysis but anchor each sub-test’s label-presence claim to the 2026-03-12 snapshot date and note current live-page status).

newsworthy: Dome eclipse battery shrinks: 4 explicitly-labelled sub-tests in 2026-03-12 snapshot collapse to 1 (E-PRED-B) by 2026-05-07. The gravity sub-test inverted — a 0.0 µGal "null confirms model" prediction was replaced by a 10–13 µGal positive-pulse prediction on the same instrument class at the same eclipse. Dome cannot have both.
